name: Mirai STEM Initiative — Cohort A Coordination
description: Coordination hub for the Mirai STEM Initiative pilot cohort. Facilitators share resources, track implementation milestones, and document outcomes across participating schools.

name: Equity Audit Working Group — Data Review
description: Working group course for reviewing disaggregated student outcome data, identifying systemic gaps, and drafting recommendations for the Board equity report.

name: FY2026-27 Budget Planning — Leadership Team
description: Restricted course for district leadership to review budget scenarios, analyze $4.8M shortfall options, and prepare board presentation materials.

name: New Principal Onboarding — Class of 2025
description: Comprehensive onboarding course for new principals joining CRUSD. Covers district policies, instructional frameworks, community engagement, and budget oversight.

name: Collective Bargaining Prep — HR and Admin
description: Internal course for HR and administration team to review bargaining proposals, compensation data, and negotiation strategy documentation ahead of union talks with CRUSD-EA.

name: Instructional Coaching — MTSS Framework PD
description: Professional development series for instructional coaches and team leads implementing MTSS across all 31 schools. Includes data protocols and intervention planning.

name: Google Workspace for Education — Staff Onboarding
description: District-wide onboarding course for staff transitioning to Google Workspace for Education Plus. Modules cover Gmail, Drive, Meet, Classroom, and security best practices.

name: Mirai STEM — Cohort B Planning
description: Planning course for the second cohort of the Mirai STEM initiative, expanding to 8 additional schools. Includes grant compliance documentation and curriculum mapping.

name: Bell Schedule Redesign Committee
description: Cross-site committee reviewing proposed bell schedule changes for the 2026-27 school year. Includes input from principals, union reps, and parent advisory groups.

name: Special Education Compliance Training — 2025
description: Mandatory professional development for special education staff, case managers, and administrators covering IDEA compliance, IEP best practices, and state reporting requirements.

name: Communications and Community Engagement PD
description: Training for principals and front-office staff on district communication standards, SchoolMessenger protocols, crisis communications, and multilingual family engagement strategies.

name: Network Infrastructure Planning — IT Team
description: Internal IT team coordination course for the district-wide fiber and WiFi upgrade project. Tracks installation schedules, vendor communications, and testing milestones.

name: Culturally Responsive Teaching — Elementary
description: Year-long professional development series on culturally responsive teaching practices for elementary educators across CRUSD. Part of the district equity action plan.

name: Board Governance and Policy Review — 2025-26
description: Resource hub for Board of Education members. Includes policy documents, board meeting materials, legal updates, and Oregon School Boards Association training modules.

name: PowerSchool SIS — Admin Certification
description: Certification training for school secretaries, registrars, and data clerks on PowerSchool SIS. Covers enrollment, grade reporting, state data submissions, and FERPA compliance.

name: PAPEA Regional Collaborative — Spring 2026
descriptionHeading: Pacific Area Principals and Educators Alliance
description: Regional learning community for PAPEA members. Hosts shared resources, cross-district case studies, and collaborative action research on equity and student outcomes.

name: Custodial and Facilities Safety Training — Annual
description: Mandatory annual safety and compliance training for all custodial, maintenance, and facilities staff. Covers OSHA standards, hazardous materials handling, and emergency procedures.

name: Literacy Across the Disciplines — Secondary PD
description: Professional development series for secondary teachers on embedding reading and writing strategies across content areas. Aligned to Oregon state literacy standards revision.

name: Cedar Bluff HS — School Improvement Plan 2025-26
description: Working course for Cedar Bluff High School leadership team to document SIP goals, track progress on student achievement targets, and compile evidence for state reporting.

name: HR Onboarding — New Hires 2025-26
description: Comprehensive orientation course for all new CRUSD employees. Covers benefits enrollment, payroll setup, district policies, code of ethics, data privacy, and mandatory reporting.

name: Mirai STEM — Grant Compliance Documentation
description: Documentation course for the Mirai STEM grant. Tracks expenditures, deliverable milestones, quarterly report submissions, and funder communications.

name: Counselor Collaborative — SEL and Crisis Protocols
description: District-wide learning community for school counselors focusing on SEL frameworks, crisis intervention protocols, and multi-tiered mental health support resources.

name: Finance and Budget Literacy for Principals
description: Professional development for principals on school-site budget management, expenditure tracking, and understanding district financial reports. Archived at end of 2024-25 cycle.

name: Dual Language Program — Teacher Certification PD
descriptionHeading: Bilingual and Dual Language Educator Training
description: Certification and ongoing professional development for teachers in the district dual language program. Covers second language acquisition theory, assessment, and family engagement.

name: Data Privacy and FERPA — Annual Recertification
description: Annual mandatory recertification for all staff with access to student data. Covers FERPA, Oregon student data privacy statutes, and district data governance protocols.
